task: A UX transformation only -- the automatic pipeline, Strategy pattern, and every algorithm module are byte-for-byte unchanged. Wraps the existing implementation in a child-friendly screen flow: Welcome -> a short magic-processing moment -> a celebratory Result screen with a before/after slider -> an optional Make It Better editing screen -> Save. Every technical control (Tolerance/Edge Smoothness/Auto Crop, zoom buttons, Overlay/Difference tabs, meta/performance readouts) stays present and fully functional in the DOM, just hidden from the child-facing UI via kids.css. Added the one genuinely new capability, a Restore Brush (Bring It Back) generalized from the existing erase-only brush via a shared paintAlphaCircle primitive, verified bit-for-bit identical to the original erase formula and confirmed to preserve RGB exactly like every other pixel. Fixed a real layout bug found while screenshotting: .workspace's old two-column grid was silently misapplied to this sprint's new children, squeezing the Result screen into a narrow column -- moved onto .edit-view, which actually has the two children that grid was built for. Brush size is three fixed choices (Small/Medium/Large) proxying the existing hidden slider. Verified end-to-end via Playwright: full flow, zero RGB mismatches through an erase+restore sequence, pre-existing regressions unchanged, zoom/wheel/resize/dark-mode all functional, zero console errors.
